Causes: Low color depth; poor fixation; light scattering at fiber surface
Recommended_Action: Apply a color-enhancing resin finish after dyeing to deepen and brighten the shade

Causes: Surface light scattering on synthetic fiber; insufficient brightening finish
Recommended_Action: Apply polishing oil by pad or spray to restore bluish deep shade and brightness

Causes: High friction between fabric and machine surfaces during wet processing; hard twist yarn rubbing
Recommended_Action: Add a dyebath lubricant to reduce friction and prevent crease/friction marks

Causes: Hard twist yarn causing friction marks; poor fabric-to-machine glide in the dyebath
Recommended_Action: Use dyebath lubricant to allow smooth fabric running and prevent crease marks

Causes: Fabric folding and rubbing during wet scouring/bleaching; lack of anti-crease lubrication
Recommended_Action: Apply wet-softening agent with anti-crease properties during scouring/bleaching

Causes: Poor dye dispersion; uneven dye exhaustion across the fabric/yarn
Recommended_Action: Add a high-concentration dispersing and leveling agent to the dyebath to equalize dye uptake

Causes: Hard twist yarn friction causing irregular dye pickup
Recommended_Action: Use dyebath lubricant to reduce friction-related dyeing irregularity

Causes: Resin/finish buildup leaving a harsh handle; no softening finish applied
Recommended_Action: Apply softening agent to restore a smooth, soft hand feel

Causes: Stiff hand feel after dyeing with no conditioning step
Recommended_Action: Apply softening agent formulated for polyester blends

Causes: Need for both softness and smoothness without affecting other properties
Recommended_Action: Apply amino-silicone based softening and smoothing finish

Causes: Residual oils/waxes on fiber surface; hydrophobic finish blocking water uptake
Recommended_Action: Apply a water-absorbing softener to restore hydrophilicity while keeping a soft hand

Causes: Lack of durable hydrophilic finish; soil/dust adhering to fiber surface
Natural oils, waxes, or sizing residues not fully removed during scouring
Recommended_Action: Use a low-foaming scouring and penetrating agent to clean the fiber surface before dyeing

Causes: Incomplete removal of spinning oils on spandex-blended fabric reducing wetting
Recommended_Action: Apply scouring agent developed specifically for spandex-blended fabrics

Causes: Inconsistent dye exhaustion rate between batches/positions in the dyebath
Recommended_Action: Use a leveling agent to equalize dye uptake and reduce batch-to-batch shade variation

Causes: Poor and uneven wetting/penetration before dyeing leading to inconsistent shade
Recommended_Action: Apply wetting and penetrating agent during desizing/scouring/bleaching for uniform preparation

Causes: Metal ion contamination (iron/calcium) reacting with peroxide and causing discoloration
Recommended_Action: Apply a sequestering agent before bleaching to chelate metal ions and prevent yellow staining

Causes: Oligomer deposits on fiber/machine surfaces causing dull, yellowish staining
Recommended_Action: Use an oligomer dispersion and removal agent (soaping step) to clear deposits
